name: Eekso — The Ritual (Boss)
dmNotes:
THE CLIMAX. Level 2 approach + the necromancer.

The whole dungeon has been plumbing, and this is the drain. Everything they've seen — the tilted floors, the warm pipes, the dated sacks of bonemeal, the tended statue of Torm — resolves in this room. Give them a beat to put it together before he speaks.

HIS CORE: he is NOT gleeful and NOT insane in a cackling way. He is devout. He took self-sacrifice — give of yourself, that is the holiest thing — and followed it one step too far: if giving your own flesh is holy, then having MORE flesh to give is holier, and other people are just... supply. He cuts himself constantly and heals with what he takes, so he can cut again. He believes he is the most generous man alive. Play him gentle, tired, and certain. That's what should scare them.

THE IMPOSTOR is the hand on his shoulder — background only. Do not explain it. One line of foreshadowing is plenty.

STAT BLOCKS STILL OWED (Wes asked): the necromancer and the big flesh summon. Running guidance is below; get the blocks from Claude before you run it.

⚑ HE HAS NO NAME YET — that's yours.
scriptText:
# The Ritual

## The Approach

The stair lets you out into a wide, low hall, and after the gallery it feels almost empty.

Almost — because the pipes came down with you. They run along the walls at knee height, all of them converging, all of them heading for the same place, and now you can hear what's in them clearly. A slow, thick, unhurried movement. Like something swallowing, over and over, taking its time.

And at the centre of the floor stands a structure that has no business being inside a building.

A great ring of stone, two storeys high, perfectly circular, sealed. There are iron gates at either end of it, shut. The pipes run to its base and disappear underneath.

Around its outside, the wall is crusted with something that grows in crystal — deep red, shot through with pink, in sprays and blooms like frost. It is warm. And it is very slightly luminous.

> [!dm] Let them circle it. Let them find the pipes going under. The room outside the ring is safe and empty on purpose — the last quiet moment they get.
>
> - **Arcana DC 15:** the crystal isn't mineral. It's grown, and it's grown out of something that was recently alive.
> - **Perception DC 12:** through the gate bars, past a gap, something inside the ring is *moving very slowly*.
> - They enter from the southeast gate. It isn't locked. If they hesitate long enough, **it opens by itself.**

## The Gates

{{music:gates}} The gate swings, and the smell comes out first — hot copper and rot and, underneath it, something almost sweet, like incense laid over a butcher's yard.

Inside, the ring is one enormous circular chamber, floored in dark slate, and the floor is cut all over with a lattice of shallow channels in some pale stone, spreading from the centre like the veins of a leaf.

The channels are full and they are running.

## The Wall

{{music:wall}} {{amb:fap-cave-whispers|vol=30%|in=6s}} And around the inside of the chamber, ten feet in from the stone, there is a wall that is not made of stone.

It stands about ten feet high, and it goes the whole way around, and it is made of blood and flesh — held up by nothing, touching nothing, hanging in the air a body's width from the wall behind it.

It is not still. It moves the way deep water moves. Things surface in it and go under again. A hand. The curve of a shoulder. A face, briefly, mouth open, and then gone.

The pipes come up through the floor at intervals all around the chamber, and they are feeding it.

> [!dm] Read it slowly. Don't add adjectives after 'a face, briefly, mouth open, and then gone' — the silence does more.
>
> - **Arcana DC 16:** the wall is being *maintained*. It is not a spell that was cast; it is a spell that is being **held**, right now, continuously.
> - Everything the party saw upstairs is in here. The stacks. The mill. The gutters. All of it arrived.

## The Symbol

And in the middle of the floor, where all the channels meet, there is a symbol thirty feet across.

The gauntlet of Torm. God of self-sacrifice. Drawn in blood, packed and raised with ground bone, so fresh in places that it is still running downhill into the grooves that were cut to carry it.

Somebody has been keeping it fresh for a very long time.

> [!dm] **Religion DC 10** — anyone who saw the statue upstairs recognises it instantly, no roll needed.
>
> The two rooms rhyme: a statue kept clean with a rag, and this. Same devotion. Same hands.
>
> This is the moment the dungeon means. Give it a beat of silence before he says anything.

## Him

{{music:him}} He's kneeling at the edge of the symbol with his back to you, and he doesn't hurry.

He's an ordinary-looking man in a plain robe, sleeves rolled to the elbow. Both forearms are a mess of scars — hundreds of them, layered, some silver with age and some from this morning. There's a small clean knife on the stone beside his knee.

He finishes what he's doing — drawing a line of his own blood along a channel with two fingers, carefully, the way you'd ice a cake — and then he sits back on his heels and looks at them over his shoulder, and he seems *pleased*.

> [!dm] Gentle. Tired. Certain. He is not performing. Deliver it quietly and don't rush the pauses.

**"Oh — you came all the way down. Most don't."**

He wipes his fingers on his robe and stands, and there's no threat in it at all.

**"Do you know what he asks for? Torm. Everyone thinks it's dying for someone. It isn't. Dying's easy, it's one afternoon and then you're finished and you've nothing left to give anybody.**

**"What he asks is that you keep giving. Every day. Of yourself. Until there's nothing left of you."**

He holds up his forearms, turns them over, and looks at them like a man showing off a garden.

**"I ran out of myself in the second year. I was thirty-one. I had nothing left to offer and years and years still to give — do you understand what a *cruelty* that is? To be willing, and empty?"**

He smiles, and it's the smile of somebody who solved a problem.

**"And then I was shown. If the giving is what's holy — then it doesn't matter whose. It only matters that it's given. And there is so *much* of it walking around out there, going to waste, being carried about by people who'll never give a drop of it to anyone."**

He gestures, almost apologetically, at the wall of red behind him.

**"They're all still giving. Every one of them. I've kept them at it for years — do you know how hard that is? How much care that takes? I don't waste anybody."**

A beat. He looks at them properly for the first time, and something moves behind his eyes that isn't his.

**"You've all got so much. You've no idea what you're carrying."**

> [!dm] **Improv handles:**
> - *"Who showed you?"* → he opens his mouth, stops, and looks genuinely confused for a second — like reaching for a word he's always known. Then he smiles and moves on. **That is the impostor. One beat. Don't explain it.**
> - *"You're insane / this is evil"* → he's not offended, he's *sad for them*. "You'd let it all rot in the ground. And you think I'm the cruel one."
> - *"We're going to stop you"* → "Yes. Probably. It's alright — it'll all still be given."
> - *Anyone mentions the people upstairs still breathing* → he brightens. He's proud of that. He explains the technique. It's the worst thing he says.
> - **He does not attack first.** He'd genuinely rather they understood. When they move, he sighs — and picks up the knife.

## The Fight

{{music:boss}}

> [!dm] ⚑ **Stat blocks owed — get them from Claude before you run this.** Design intent so you can improvise if you must:
>
> **The clock is the fight.** He summons; they have to put him down before the summons put them down. Everything below serves that.
>
> - **He pays in his own flesh.** Every summon costs him hit points — he opens his arm and something stands up out of the floor. That's the mockery of Torm made mechanical: his self-sacrifice IS the threat. Damaging him and racing his summons are the same clock.
> - **The wall heals him.** While the wall stands and the pipes run, he regains hit points every round. This is the lever: the party can attack the pipes or the wall itself to shut it off. **Reward that fast** — the moment a player thinks of it, make it visibly work.
> - **The summons:** one big flesh thing to start (Large, slow, grabby, hits hard), a second if the fight runs long. They come up out of the blood-channels, not from nowhere.
> - **Budget:** ~6,000–6,700 XP against 4x lvl 6 + Cumb is a proper high-difficulty climax. Necromancer around CR 8 with two CR 3–4 summons lands there.
>
> {{music:summon}} **When the first one stands up**, cut the talking. It should take a full round of nobody understanding what they're looking at.
>
> **Endgame:** when he drops, the wall loses whatever was holding it. It comes down all at once — thirty feet of standing blood hitting a stone floor. Everything in the room is soaked. Nobody is getting clean.

## After

{{music:after}}

> [!dm] Land it quietly. No fanfare.
>
> - The channels stop running. The room goes quiet in a way it clearly hasn't been in years.
> - Upstairs, in the stacks, the ones who were still breathing stop. They are the ones who die peacefully in this dungeon.
> - The knife is ordinary. Cheap, worn thin from sharpening. He'd had it a long time.
> - **His forearms:** the oldest scars are neat and evenly spaced. The recent ones are not. Somewhere in the middle, the handwriting changed.
>
> ⚑ **Threads for you:** what the impostor wanted out of this, whether the wall was building toward something, and whether anything here connects to the pool. All open — nothing in this scene forces an answer tonight.

ideas.text: If they hesitate at the gate too long, it opens by itself
Arcana: the wall isn't a spell that was cast, it's a spell being HELD, continuously, right now
He draws his own blood along the channel with two fingers, carefully, like icing a cake
'I ran out of myself in the second year. I was thirty-one.'
Ask who showed him and he blanks for a second — reaching for a word he's always known. The impostor. One beat only.
The oldest scars on his arms are neat and evenly spaced; the recent ones aren't. Somewhere in the middle the handwriting changed.
When he dies the wall drops all at once — thirty feet of standing blood hitting stone. Nobody gets clean.
If asked about the people upstairs still breathing, he brightens and explains the technique. Worst thing he says.

entities.name: The necromancer ⚑ unnamed, block owed
entities.note: Devout, gentle, certain. Twisted Torm's self-sacrifice. Pays HP to summon; healed by the wall. ~CR 8.

entities.name: The wall of blood and flesh
entities.note: Ten feet high, hovering a body's width off the stone, fed by the pipes. Heals him each round — destroying it is the party's lever.

entities.name: The gauntlet of Torm, in blood and bone
entities.note: Thirty feet across, kept fresh. Rhymes with the tended statue in the foyer.

entities.name: Flesh summon ⚑ block owed
entities.note: Large, slow, grabby. Rises out of the blood-channels. Second one if the fight runs long.

entities.name: The impostor's hand
entities.note: One beat of confusion when asked who showed him. Do not explain.
